instructions: Do not remove feature entries from this file unless the product scope has genuinely changed.
Treat `passes` as the source of truth for completion.
Only mark `passes` as true after the listed automated and/or manual verification succeeds.
Keep only metadata in this JSON; edit the referenced `harness/features/MF-XXX.md` file for notes, steps, and verification prose.

features.title: Headings render in WYSIWYG mode without mutating the markdown source
Inline emphasis, inline code, and strikethrough hide syntax away from the caret
Fenced code blocks render safely and preserve fenced markdown source
Switching between WYSIWYG and source modes preserves document text, selection, and undo history
List markers and blockquotes render contextually in WYSIWYG mode
Smart list continuation and exit behavior work on Enter
Opening a markdown file from the desktop shell hydrates the editor state and active file path
Save and Save As write the live editor buffer rather than the starter template
New file flow clears document state, dirty state, and represented filename cleanly
Rendered links are clickable and open externally from the desktop shell
Images render as inline previews while preserving markdown source
Theme engine hot-reloads CSS themes and lets the user switch themes
Tables render in WYSIWYG mode and stay editable as markdown
Math blocks render through KaTeX or MathJax without corrupting source markup
Export pipeline can generate HTML and PDF from the active document
Plugins can register markdown post-processors for custom render transforms
Opt-in Yjs collaboration can bootstrap a shared document session
Main branch merges produce installable desktop beta builds automatically
Focus mode and typewriter mode keep attention on the active writing block
Outline panel shows heading hierarchy and supports quick navigation in long documents
A `[toc]` block expands into a live table of contents that stays in sync with headings
Footnote references render cleanly and reveal editable note content without corrupting markdown
Mermaid and other diagram fences render live previews while preserving fenced source
Horizontal rules render as separators in WYSIWYG mode without losing the underlying markdown
YAML front matter remains intact and editable as document metadata
Smart paste converts rich clipboard content into markdown while preserving plain-text paste
Word count surfaces live document and selection stats without treating markdown syntax as words
Find and replace can search the active document without leaving the editing flow
Dragging, pasting, or inserting local images creates markdown image references without breaking asset paths
Fenced code blocks display language-aware syntax highlighting in both WYSIWYG and source modes
Smart typography auto-converts ASCII punctuation to typographic equivalents as the user types
Auto-save periodically writes the live buffer to disk so no work is lost on unexpected quit
Open Quickly fuzzy-searches files in the current folder and recent locations
Admonition and callout blocks render with distinct icons and colors while preserving blockquote markdown
Split view shows live source and rendered preview side-by-side and keeps them synchronized
Heading sections can be folded and unfolded to hide document subtrees while editing
Superscript and subscript syntax renders inline without mutating source
Inline HTML blocks pass through the WYSIWYG renderer without being escaped or stripped
Spell checking underlines misspelled words in prose and offers inline correction suggestions
Wikilinks (`[[page]]`) resolve to internal document links and navigate within the active vault
Vault sidebar shows a file tree for the active folder and supports open, rename, and delete
Vim keybinding mode provides modal editing with normal, insert, and visual commands
Reading mode renders the document as fully static HTML without an editable code surface
Highlight spans (`==text==`) render inline without mutating source
Global search queries content across files in the opened folder and jumps to exact matches
Export pipeline integrates with Pandoc to generate DOCX, EPUB, and LaTeX artifacts from the active document
Paragraph shortcuts convert the current line between heading levels 1-6 and plain paragraph text
Virtual rendering limits DOM nodes to a viewport window for files over 5 000 lines
Incremental parsing updates only dirty AST ranges instead of re-parsing the whole document
Background indexer builds a symbol table for headings and anchors without blocking the UI thread
Outline panel lists all headings with live scroll-sync and click-to-jump navigation
Chunk-based file loader streams large files in 64 KB chunks to avoid blocking the main thread on open
Fuzzy document-wide search highlights all matches and jumps between them with keyboard shortcuts
Find-and-replace supports regex mode, case-sensitivity toggle, and whole-word matching
Memory-mapped read path allows random-access navigation in files larger than available RAM
Go-to-line dialog jumps the caret and viewport to an arbitrary line number instantly
Multi-cursor editing allows placing cursors on non-contiguous lines and editing them simultaneously
Column (block) selection mode lets users select a rectangular region across multiple lines
Undo history is capped at 500 steps and compressed for large-file sessions to stay under 256 MB
Auto-save writes a recovery checkpoint every 30 seconds without blocking the editor
Lazy image loading defers off-screen image decoding until the image enters the viewport
Folding collapses any block element (heading section, code block, list) to a single summary line
Minimap shows a scaled-down glyph view of the full document and supports click-to-scroll
Increase/Decrease Heading Level shortcuts promote or demote the current heading by one level
Paragraph shortcuts convert the current line into quote, ordered-list, and unordered-list blocks
Style shortcuts wrap the current selection for bold, italic, underline, and links without leaving the editor flow
Alternate LaTeX delimiters `\(...\)` and `\[...\]` render as math in WYSIWYG mode
Table editing hotkeys support row/column insertion and row movement without raw markdown surgery
Rendered markdown tables support interactive column resizing with persisted alignment
Image insertion honors Typora-style destination/root preferences for dropped and pasted assets
Spellcheck settings allow language selection and custom dictionary behavior in-editor
Image drag-to-resize handles update width/height in markdown source
Emoji shortcode autocomplete expands `:name:` into Unicode glyphs
Command palette (Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+P) invokes any editor or desktop action by name
Copy writes rich clipboard formats, while Copy as Markdown and Copy as HTML Code expose source formats
Paste as plain text shortcut strips rich formatting before insertion
Clear formatting command removes inline markup from the current selection
Move line and selection up/down shortcut reorders blocks without clipboard usage
Duplicate line or selection shortcut inserts a copy below without touching the clipboard
Auto-pair brackets, quotes, backticks, and markdown delimiters on insertion
Image upload integration sends pasted/dropped images through a PicGo-compatible uploader
Preferences dialog exposes editor, theme, export, and shortcut settings
Reveal in Finder / Show in folder action opens the active file's location
Fullscreen and distraction-free modes hide window chrome and side panels
Heading auto-numbering toggle renders 1., 1.1, 1.2 prefixes via CSS counters
Math parity covers Typora-style code-block math, equation numbering, line breaks, and TeX package support
Flowchart, sequence, gantt, block, Venn, and Ishikawa diagrams render from Typora-style fences
Document tabs keep multiple markdown files open in a single window
UI localization and RTL document direction support Typora-style language workflows
Open Recent tracks recent files and folders, with clear-history and pinned-location behavior
Always-on-top window toggle persists per-window across sessions
Back and forward navigation history jumps between visited headings and files
Source mode displays a configurable line-number gutter
Insert date/time action writes the current timestamp in user-selected format
Document statistics panel surfaces words, characters, reading time, and per-selection counts
Custom keybinding editor lets users remap any palette-registered command
Embedded HTML media tags render preserved `<video>`, `<audio>`, and `<iframe>` content in WYSIWYG mode
HTML comments and HTML entities are preserved in WYSIWYG mode without leaking raw markup
Auto pair brackets, quotes, and markdown delimiters wrap selections and preserve typing context
Strict mode enforces GFM-style heading whitespace and list indentation rules
Delete range commands remove word, sentence or line, block, and styled scope with Typora-style semantics
Zoom in, zoom out, and reset zoom adjust the window scale without disturbing document state
Launch behavior can reopen the last file/folder or open a configured default folder on startup
System dark mode applies separate light and dark themes automatically
Enter creates a new paragraph while Shift+Enter inserts a single line break with Typora-style source mapping
Paragraph shortcuts insert table, code fence, and math block scaffolds without raw markdown typing
Export pipeline renders the active document to PDF and HTML from the desktop menu
Unified preferences dialog consolidates editor, theme, export, spellcheck, and launch settings
App shell module decomposition shrinks App.tsx into focused feature hooks and components
Feature ledger storage is normalized so long-form notes live in per-feature files
Accessibility audit hardens palette, search, sidebar, and floating toolbar dialogs
Performance budget guards editor open-to-interactive and keystroke latency on large documents
Windows and Linux desktop packaging ships from CI alongside macOS builds
Auto-update channel delivers signed beta builds on every main merge
Superscript and subscript inline render unblocks MF-037
Markdown parser receives property-based fuzz coverage for strict and tolerant modes
Theme hot-reload watches user CSS files and swaps variables without restarting the editor
Plugin API beta exposes commands, markdown post-processors, and custom views
Repo root cleanup removes stray one-shot scripts and legacy artifacts
Contributor documentation covers architecture, harness protocol, and release flow
Central command registry backs the command palette, menus, and custom keybindings
Rendered markdown table cells wrap long and multiline content instead of eliding it
Saving an untitled new file prompts for the target folder and filename
Split view preview pane always shows raw `#` on the first heading
Split view scroll positions are desynced on load
Split view content sync replaces the entire preview document on every keystroke
toggleViewMode (Mod+/) jumps to WYSIWYG instead of Source when in Reading or Split mode
Document search shows "1 matches" (grammar) and flashes "0 matches" before worker responds
Outline panel collapse state is not persisted across sessions
Status bar shows stale cursor line number after tab is loaded from a snapshot
Outline toggle button appears in titlebar when sidebar already shows the outline
Export editor instance is editable and missing onDocumentEdit
Mac detection uses deprecated navigator.platform
Closing the last tab briefly sets activeTabId to null
Global search opens with no explanation when no vault folder is open
Double-clicking an image opens a moveable floating lightbox window
Pandoc import converts DOCX, RTF, EPUB, OPML, and other supported formats into editable markdown
Export presets remember previous targets and can be customized with YAML-aware options
File sidebar supports Typora-style Articles/File List mode, refresh, sort, move, duplicate, copy path, and undo file operations
Markdown auto-pair wraps selections and completes Typora-style inline delimiters
Export and print honor Typora-style manual and heading-based page breaks
Image context actions delete, move, copy, and relink document image assets
Export pipeline renders the active document to a Typora-style image
